posts.hook: These Arctic dinosaurs lived where temperatures hit -40°F. Scientists just found them.
slides: These Arctic dinosaurs lived where temperatures hit -40°F. Scientists just found them.
Meet Ugrunaaluk — 69 million years old, discovered in Alaska's frozen ground
They had specialized blood vessels to survive polar winters
Walk through their ice cave habitat. Touch real fossils.
Ice Dinosaurs opens this weekend. Get tickets before we sell out.
posts.caption:
Scientists thought dinosaurs couldn't survive Arctic conditions. Then they found Ugrunaaluk.

This 30-foot herbivore lived in Alaska when temperatures dropped to -40°F. How? Specialized blood flow and seasonal migration patterns we're still studying.

See the evidence yourself. Walk through recreated ice caves. Touch 69-million-year-old fossils.

Ice Dinosaurs. Intelligence, Elevated.
posts.visual_direction: Slide 1: Dramatic close-up of frozen dinosaur skull in ice. Slide 2: Full Ugrunaaluk skeleton against aurora backdrop. Slide 3: Scientific diagram showing blood vessel adaptations. Slide 4: Kids touching fossils at interactive station. Slide 5: Exhibition entrance with ticket counter, people queuing

posts.hook: Your kid thinks they know dinosaurs. They don't know these ones.
slides: Your kid thinks they know dinosaurs. They don't know these ones.
These lived in snow. Had feathers. Migrated like birds.
Found in 2021. Never displayed anywhere until now.
Watch their faces when they see the 40-foot ice cave recreation
Ice Dinosaurs tickets selling fast. Weekend slots almost gone.
posts.caption:
T-Rex? Basic. Triceratops? Everyone knows those.

These Arctic dinosaurs were discovered 3 years ago. Your kids have never seen them in any book, movie, or museum.

Feathered giants that survived polar nights. Migration patterns that changed everything we thought we knew.

Be the parent who shows them something completely new.

Ice Dinosaurs. This weekend only.
posts.visual_direction: Slide 1: Child's shocked expression looking up. Slide 2: Feathered dinosaur model in snowy environment. Slide 3: Paleontologist with 2021 discovery tools and fossils. Slide 4: Kids walking through massive ice cave with aurora ceiling. Slide 5: Nearly empty ticket availability screen

posts.hook: Scientists found dinosaur fossils with ice still in their bones.
slides: Scientists found dinosaur fossils with ice still in their bones.
Preserved for 69 million years in Arctic permafrost
Blood vessels intact. Soft tissue visible under microscope.
See the actual specimens. Touch the discovery tools.
Ice Dinosaurs — experience the science that's rewriting textbooks
posts.caption:
Permafrost preservation changed everything.

69 million years. Blood vessels still visible. Soft tissue samples extracted from bone marrow.

This isn't reconstruction. This is the actual evidence that's forcing scientists to rewrite dinosaur biology textbooks.

Touch the tools. See the specimens. Understand the discovery process.

Ice Dinosaurs opens Saturday.
posts.visual_direction: Slide 1: Microscopic view of preserved blood vessels in fossil. Slide 2: Scientist extracting ice-preserved fossil from permafrost. Slide 3: Lab setup with actual soft tissue samples under microscope. Slide 4: Interactive station with real excavation tools. Slide 5: Exhibition logo with dramatic ice cave background

posts.hook: Wait for it... this dinosaur lived where it was -40°F

posts.caption:
Everyone thinks dinosaurs lived in hot, tropical climates.

Wait for it...

These Arctic giants survived -40°F temperatures. Had specialized blood flow. Migrated thousands of miles.

Discovered 2021. Displayed nowhere else.

Ice Dinosaurs. This weekend.
